name: Chocolate Mint Terrine
recipeInstructions:
Make the ganache coating:
1. Place a 8 1/2-by-4 1/2-by-2 3/4-inch non-stick metal loaf pan in the freezer while preparing the ganache coating.
2. In a medium bowl, place the chocolate.
3. In a small saucepan over medium heat, bring cream to gentle boil. Pour hot cream over chocolate. Let mixture stand for 30 seconds and gently whisk until smooth. Stir in creme de menthe.
4. Remove pan from freezer. Pour half ganache into pan. Rotate pan slowly while allowing ganache to coat the inside of the pan evenly over bottom and sides. Allow excess ganache to drip back into bowl. Return pan to freezer for 5 minutes.
5. Remove pan from freezer, spread additional ganache only on sides with small metal spatula. Return pan to freezer. Cover remaining ganache with plastic wrap.
Make the dark chocolate mousse:
1. Melt chocolate and butter together; set aside to cool.
2. In chilled large bowl, whip cream until soft mounds form. Refrigerate until needed.
3. In 4 1/2-quart electric mixer bowl, beat eggs, 1 tablespoon water, and salt over medium speed until blended. Add sugar gradually while beating. Remove from mixer stand, place over hot water bath (bottom must touch water). Cook whisking constantly for 3 to 5 minutes or until mixture reaches 140 degrees F on instant-read thermometer. Return bowl to mixer stand; beat at medium-high speed until thick and pale yellow, about 8 minutes.
4. In small heatproof cup, sprinkle gelatin over 2 tablespoons water. Let soften for 5 minutes.
5. Place softened gelatin in saucepan with enough hot water to come halfway up side of cup. Heat mixture over not simmering water; stir frequently for 3 to 4 minutes until gelatin dissolves and is clear. Remove from heat, leave cup in hot water to keep warm.
6. Add gelatin to egg mixture, whisk gently by hand. Fold one-third whipped cream into egg mixture to lighten it. Fold remaining whipped cream.
Make the mint mousse:
1. Whip cream in chilled large bowl until soft mounds form; refrigerate until needed.
2. In 4 1/2-quart electric mixer bowl with wire whip attachment, beat eggs and yolk over medium speed until blended. Add sugar gradually while beating. Beat in creme de menthe.
3. Place egg mixture over hot water bath (bottom must touch water). Cook whisking constantly for 3 to 5 minutes or until reaches 140 degrees F on instant-read thermometer. Return bowl to mixer stand; beat at medium-high speed until thick and pale yellow, about 8 minutes.
4. Sprinkle gelatin over remaining tablespoons water. Let soften for 5 minutes.
5. Place softened gelatin in saucepan with enough hot water to come halfway up side of cup. Heat mixture over not simmering water; stir frequently for 3 to 4 minutes until gelatin dissolves and is clear. Remove from heat, leave cup in hot water.
Assemble the terrine:
1. Remove loaf pan from freezer. Pour one-third dark chocolate mousse into pan and spread evenly. Return pan to freezer.
2. Set remaining dark chocolate mousse aside in cool place.
3. Make mint mousse as directed above.
4. Assemble terrine: Pour half mint mousse over chocolate mousse layer, spreading level with offset metal spatula. Return pan to freezer for 5 minutes.
5. Pour thin layer of chocolate mousse over mint mousse, spread evenly. Return pan to freezer for 5 minutes. Pour remaining mint mousse over chocolate layer and spread evenly. Return pan to freezer for 10 minutes. Remove from freezer; pour enough chocolate mousse over mint mousse to fill pan to same level as ganache coating. Refrigerate reserved chocolate mousse; cover pan with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate terrine until ready to unmold:
1. Cut cardboard and foil to fit filling surface. Press paper onto filling.
2. Dip top rim of pan in hot water for 20 seconds, remove and wipe dry.
3. Place foil-covered cardboard over pan, carefully invert. Remove pan from terrine; smooth sides and top with metal spatula.
4. Place terrine on wire rack set over baking sheet. Pour reserved ganache over terrine, covering completely. Refrigerate until glaze is set.
Final assembly:
1. Fill pastry bag fitted with star tip (such as Ateco #5) with reserved chocolate mousse. Pipe rosettes along length of terrine and around base.
2. Garnish with mint leaves. Refrigerate before serving.
